response:
1. The width of the flower bed is $ x $, so its length is $ 2x $. The area of the flower bed is $ x \times 2x = 2x^2 $. The walkway adds a uniform width $ w $ around the flower bed, increasing the total dimensions. The total width becomes $ x + 2w $, and the total length becomes $ 2x + 2w $. The total area of the flower bed and walkway is $ (x + 2w)(2x + 2w) $.

2. The area of the flower bed and walkway is $ (x + 2w)(2x + 2w) $. Substituting $ x = 6 $, the area becomes $ (6 + 2w)(12 + 2w) $. The total area is $ 72 $ square feet more than the flower bed alone. The area of the flower bed is $ 2x^2 = 2 \times 36 = 72 $ square feet. The total area of the flower bed and walkway is $ 72 + 72 = 144 $ square feet. Solving $ (6 + 2w)(12 + 2w) = 144 $, we find $ w = 2 $ feet.
